payload.idea:
In regards to the View Ticket dialog, I have some feedback I want to address

Reduce visual density and improve hierarchy
The screen is extremely information-dense, especially in the ticket body and right sidebar. A UI/UX engineer would likely ask for stronger spacing, typography hierarchy, and grouping to make scanning easier and reduce cognitive load.
Make the primary action clearer
“Resume Implementation” is visually dominant, but it’s unclear whether it resumes an AI agent, editing mode, or a workflow state. The primary CTA should communicate intent more explicitly (e.g. “Resume AI Agent”, “Continue Work”, “Resume Session”).
Convert the Details sidebar into editable inline fields
The right panel feels form-heavy and disconnected from the workflow. A UX engineer may request inline-editable chips/dropdowns directly under the title or in a compact metadata row to reduce sidebar dependency.
Improve readability of the ticket description
The long implementation notes are difficult to parse. Suggestions would likely include:
collapsible sections
markdown rendering improvements
code references with syntax styling
checklist formatting
automatic grouping by “Finding”, “Implementation”, “Risk”, etc.
Redesign the Agent Activity panel
The current panel exposes low-level operational telemetry (“events”, “files edited”, “thread id”) that feels overly technical for a kanban workflow. A UX engineer would probably request:
a cleaner activity timeline
human-readable progress updates
simplified metrics
progressive disclosure for advanced diagnostics
Improve contrast and accessibility
Several text areas use low-contrast gray-on-dark combinations that are difficult to read. Accessibility improvements would likely include:
higher contrast text
larger default font size
clearer focus states
keyboard navigation visibility
WCAG compliance improvements
Introduce resizable or collapsible panels
The ticket content area feels cramped because the right sidebar consumes substantial width. A UX engineer would likely request:
collapsible sidebar
draggable panel resizing
focus mode for reading/editing tickets
Improve the refinement workflow UX
The “Refine Ticket” section currently feels disconnected and inactive. Improvements might include:
suggested prompts/examples
quick actions (“Add acceptance criteria”, “Generate checklist”, “Request architecture review”)
inline AI assistance chips
visible draft state and feedback indicators
Clarify status indicators and execution states
The top-left badges (“Running”, “Ready”) create ambiguity because both appear simultaneously. A UI/UX engineer would likely request:
clearer mutually exclusive states
consistent terminology
status tooltips
timeline/progress visualization showing what is actively happening
Modernize the kanban ticket interaction model
This feels more like a technical console than a workboard card. UX improvements could include:
breadcrumb/context showing board → column → ticket
related tickets/dependencies preview
activity/comment tabs
sticky action bar
quick navigation between tickets without opening a heavy full-screen dialog

payload.title: Reduce density and improve View Ticket dialog hierarchy
